codeReview.summary: The feature delivers the renderer, demo runner, and broad test coverage, but one requirement is not fully implemented: the heuristic policy does not consume observation alerts when choosing fault-specific remediation.

issues.description: HeuristicAgent.act only inspects state.active_incidents (line 71) and never reads obs.active_alerts before deciding actions. The feature contract requires using active_incidents/alerts from the observation to determine fault type. In a state with alert text indicating bad_config but no active_incidents, this implementation returns NoOp instead of rollback/restart.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission shared docs disagree on demo module location/entrypoint: AGENTS.md documents demo under sre_agent_sandbox/demo, but services.yaml defines demo execution as `python -m demo.run_demo`, which requires a top-level demo package. Worker added a compatibility wrapper to reconcile this.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md:40-42 shows `sre_agent_sandbox/.../demo/run_demo.py`; .factory/services.yaml:7 runs `uv run python -m demo.run_demo`; commit 5e8ddab adds demo/run_demo.py wrapper.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The server-worker skill does not mention the top-level `demo.run_demo` invocation path expected by services.yaml, which led to an implementation workaround and explicit skill feedback.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md:35-36,48 describe demo implementation but not module-path compatibility; handoff ...bfde52cb....json:181-183 suggests adding guidance for `python -m demo.run_demo` support.

summary: Reviewed commit 5e8ddab, handoff, transcript skeleton, and skill/docs. Marked FAIL due to a blocking requirement gap: heuristic decision logic ignores observation alerts when determining fault type.
